channel.title: AP News 全文 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: 泰国考古遗址挖掘过程中发现距今约2000年的金戒指
item.description:
曼谷（美联社）——官员称，在泰国西部一处新考古遗址的挖掘过程中，发现了两枚距今约 2000 年的金戒指。
泰国政府美术部在一份声明中表示，上周在碧武里府 Don Yai Thong 考古遗址进行的挖掘工作中，发现了这些带有人骨的戒指。
周四发现的一枚戒指上刻有据信是婆罗米文字（一种古印度书写系统）的字符。该部门表示，专家的初步评估确定该文字为“pusarakhitasa”，意思是“受普希亚保护的人”，据说是印度天文学中最吉祥的生肖之一。
与相同骨骼遗骸一起发现的另一枚戒指是一枚纯金戒指，没有任何图案。该部门表示，专家认为戒指的主人可能是印度古代种姓制度吠舍的一名商人。
位于首都曼谷西南约 130 公里（80 英里）处的 Don Yai Thong 考古遗址于今年年初被发现，当时居民在稻田中发现了古代铜鼓碎片，并导致进一步挖掘。
该遗址的历史可以追溯到泰国史前时代晚期，这是一个人类定居时期，也称为铁器时代，距今约 1,500 至 2,500 年前。
自二月以来，考古学家发现了八具人体骨骼、青铜和黄金珠宝、陶器和其他文物，表明富人或社会上层阶级的成员进行了仪式性埋葬。
美术部表示，挖掘工作预计将在一个月内完成，并计划向公众展示考古发现。

item.title: 哈马斯解散加沙政府，将权力移交给联合国支持的委员会
item.description:
加沙地带代尔巴拉赫（美联社）——哈马斯激进组织周一表示，已解散加沙政府，并准备将权力移交给联合国支持的技术委员会，作为美国斡旋停火协议的一部分。
哈马斯没有透露是否计划采取解除武装或将安全移交给国际部队的关键步骤，但将其决定描述为其致力于多年战争后加沙重建的证据。
目前尚不清楚下级官员宣布的这一举措是否会带来任何有意义的变化。
由唐纳德·特朗普总统领导的新实体和平委员会负责管理和重建加沙，该委员会表示了解哈马斯的声明，但将根据“行动而不是承诺”评估其影响。该委员会在 X 日的一份声明中强调，技术专家委员会必须按照停火协议的规定控制加沙的所有武器。
在周一的新闻发布会上，哈马斯管理的政府媒体办公室主任伊斯梅尔·塔瓦卜塔表示，“只有技术和专业人员”将继续留在岗位上，管理巴勒斯坦飞地的日常事务。
塔瓦卜塔在代尔巴拉赫阿克萨医院院子里举行的新闻发布会上说：“所有提供服务的员工都是‘国家雇员’，并已做好在加沙国家管理委员会领导下工作的充分准备。”哈马斯发言人哈齐姆·卡西姆称其为“在执行停火协议的道路上向前迈出的积极一步”。
以色列外交部长吉迪恩·萨尔驳斥了这一举动，称此举是为了避免裁军。“只要哈马斯保留其武器，任何文职政府当然都会按照哈马斯的指示行事，”他在 X 上写道。
该技术官僚委员会总部设在开罗，主席为出生于加沙的工程师、巴勒斯坦权力机构前官员阿里·沙阿斯 (Ali Shaath)。它的任务是在联合国和和平委员会的监督下恢复基本服务并监督民政事务。
在 X 的一份声明中，沙阿斯承认了哈马斯周一的声明，并表示，为了使该委员会有效运作，必须有“一个在一个法律框架下运作的单一管理机构”和“一个对该机构负责的统一安全机构”。
停火协议签署九个月后，以色列和哈马斯之间的谈判在第二阶段的实施方面仍基本陷入僵局，包括哈马斯解除武装和加沙重建。
哈马斯坚持先实施第一阶段，然后再讨论其武器。
2023 年 10 月 7 日，哈马斯领导的武装分子发起的袭击引发了战争，造成以色列约 1,200 人死亡，另有 251 人被劫为人质。据加沙卫生部称，以色列在加沙的报复性攻势已造成 73,098 名巴勒斯坦人死亡。
该部门是哈马斯领导的政府的一部分，配备了医疗专业人员，并保存着联合国机构和独立专家认为普遍可靠的详细记录。它没有区分平民和武装分子，但表示妇女和儿童约占所有死亡人数的一半。
自 10 月 10 日停火生效以来，以色列的袭击已大大减少，但几乎每天都在继续。以色列军方表示，他们的目标是哈马斯和其他武装分子，并经常声称他们正在策划袭击。袭击还造成许多平民死亡。
卫生官员称，周一，以色列的空袭造成加沙至少五人死亡，其中三人在南部汗尤尼斯，两人在加沙城一间公寓内。
以色列军方表示，他们在加沙城袭击中针对的是一名哈马斯特工，在汗尤尼斯袭击中针对的是巴勒斯坦伊斯兰圣战组织的一名武装分子。
武装分子对加沙地带的以色列军队进行了枪击袭击，自停火以来已有五名以色列士兵死亡。___
美联社驻耶路撒冷撰稿人梅兰妮·利德曼对此报道做出了贡献。

item.title: 伊朗悼念哈梅内伊，油轮在霍尔木兹海峡被炮弹击中起火
item.description:
阿拉伯联合酋长国迪拜（美联社）——英国军方表示，周二凌晨，一艘在霍尔木兹海峡阿曼海岸航行的油轮在被炮弹击中后起火。
这次袭击是针对一艘穿过波斯湾狭窄河口的船只的最新一次袭击，和平时期五分之一的石油和天然气交易都经过该海湾。伊朗国家电视台称，这艘液化天然气油轮在无视警告后遭到袭击，但并未直接声称对此次袭击负责。
德黑兰多次宣称，只有其批准的通过该海峡的路线是安全的，并涉嫌攻击其他使用靠近阿曼海岸的另一条路线的船只。
美国急于推进与伊朗的谈判，旨在全面重新开放该海峡，遏制德黑兰有争议的核计划，并永久结束2月28日发动的战争。但此前该海峡发生的袭击事件引发了美国的报复性打击，随后伊朗袭击了海湾阿拉伯国家，这增加了局势升级的风险。
与此同时，伊朗和美国之间的谈判似乎暂停，直到埋葬伊朗已故最高领袖哈梅内伊（哈梅内伊在战争开始时被杀）。越来越多的迹象表明，参加他葬礼的哀悼者呼吁美国总统唐纳德·特朗普去世。
当局连夜将哈梅内伊的遗体空运到什叶派神学院城市库姆，周二哀悼者在那里悼念他。
英国海事贸易运营中心表示，这艘油轮在阿曼利马海峡附近被击中。UKMTO表示，当该船试图向南驶出海峡前往阿曼湾时，射弹击中了该船的左舷。
据称，此次罢工没有对环境造成影响，当局正在调查。
伊朗国家电视台援引匿名消息人士的话说，德黑兰对一艘据称从卡塔尔运载天然气的油轮实施了袭击。然而，伊斯兰共和国尚未就此次袭击事件发表正式声明。
伊朗联合军事指挥部上周四警告称，所有通过该海峡的油轮必须使用其批准的路线。声明还表示，美军在该海峡的干涉“将遭到迅速、果断的反应”。
但由美国海军监管的跨国机构联合海事信息中心周一告诉托运人，阿曼周围的航线“已经扩大，仍然可供所有交通使用”。
特朗普周一在白宫还警告伊朗，他们需要“达成协议，否则我们将完成这项工作”。
特朗普表示：“我宁愿达成协议，因为我不想影响 9100 万人。”“我们可以在一小时内拆除他们的桥梁。我们可以切断他们的能源供应。”
作为临时协议的一部分，伊朗和美国同意允许船只在 60 天内免付费用通过。但德黑兰坚称必须控制船只的航线，并随后收取通行费，这颠覆了该水道数十年​​的做法。
美国和许多海湾阿拉伯国家表示，他们不会同意伊朗收取通过该海峡的费用。阿曼和联合国机构早些时候试图在阿曼海岸附近开辟一条新航线，引发了整个中东地区的袭击，凸显了紧张局势。
数据公司 Kpler 报告称，上周末至少有 108 艘船只通过不同航线穿越该海峡。
伊朗国家电视台周二早些时候播出了一架直升机的现场图像，数十万人步行前往库姆以南的贾姆卡兰清真寺，参加哈梅内伊的葬礼。什叶派认为这座清真寺曾接待过穆罕默德·马赫迪，他是第十二位也是最后一位什叶派伊玛目，他于 9 世纪失踪，有一天会重新出现，为世界带来正义。
哀悼者举着的横幅和海报上展示了哈梅内伊和他的儿子、伊朗新任最高领袖阿亚图拉·莫杰塔巴·哈梅内伊的照片。莫吉塔巴·哈梅内伊尚未出现在几天后举行的葬礼上。据报道，他在导致他父亲死亡的空袭中受伤，据信他目前躲藏起来。
在战争最激烈的时候，在四月份停火之前，以色列把目标瞄准了伊朗最高领导人，至少在一个案例中，以色列很可能利用他们的公开露面来巩固自己的立场。它还威胁要杀死年轻的哈梅内伊。
当局已关闭街道、领空和日常生活，哀悼活动从周六开始，周四结束，哈梅内伊被埋葬在他的出生地马什哈德的伊玛目礼萨圣陵。哈梅内伊86岁。

item.title: ICE 通过电子邮件派警员前往一名男子的家中。现在他正在起诉
item.description:
纽约州北部的一名居民起诉美国移民和海关执法局，称其派联邦官员到他家，并通过他发给该机构前任负责人的电子邮件发出警告。
美国公民大卫·斯特里弗 (David Streever) 的律师称，今年 6 月，两名警官出现在他位于罗切斯特的家中，并向他的妻子出示了一份警告通知，告知他几个月前发送的电子邮件被视为威胁。斯特里弗于 1 月份向时任 ICE 代理主任托德·莱昂斯 (Todd Lyons) 发送了这封电子邮件，此前一名移民官员在一次反 ICE 示威活动中射杀了明尼阿波利斯居民蕾妮·古德 (Renee Good)。
在电子邮件中，斯特里弗称里昂是“一个可怕的人”，“永远不知道和平”。他表示，该机构周一在华盛顿特区提起诉讼，侵犯了他的第一修正案权利。
斯特里弗是纽约州北部至少两名因在网上批评 ICE 后收到联邦警告的居民之一。总部位于费城的个人权利与表达基金会代表斯特里弗，并表示提起诉讼是因为斯特里弗的言论自由权受到侵犯。
“这显然受到第一修正案的保护，”该基金会的律师亚当·斯坦博 (Adam Steinbaugh) 说。“这是在政治演讲的背景下。”
ICE 的代表此前拒绝就向斯特里弗发出的警告发表评论，理由是调查正在进行，该机构周一也没有立即发表评论。该诉讼还点名了国土安全部部长马克韦恩·穆林，他的办公室发表了一份声明，称“任何关于国土安全部及其下属机构试图‘压制’言论自由的指控都是绝对错误的。”
声明称：“任何袭击或威胁我们执法人员的人都将面临后果。”
这封三段电子邮件的全部内容都以“下一步是什么”为主题，并提到了纳粹德国的一名领导人：“你是一个可怕的人，将作为美国的屠夫莱因哈德·海德里希载入史册。
“即使我们看到这些视频，你保护明尼苏达州明显执行的方式也会导致你的垮台。就连特朗普也会在最后一刻对你发起攻击，而你将成为一个悲伤的、被鄙视的人，为自己可悲的弱点感到羞愧，活活吃掉自己。
“你永远不会平静。你会寻求迷失自己，逃避了解自己真相的负担。但无论你走到哪里，你都会找到自己。你会折磨自己，直到你在地球上的最后一天。”
斯坦博说，当斯特里弗从芬兰回来时，联邦特工还试图在纽约市的一家酒店与他对峙，但被酒店工作人员拒绝了。
就在纽约初选期间，联邦官员前往斯特里弗家的同一周，官员们在纽约初选期间的一个投票地点拜访了民意调查工作人员佩吉琳·贡耶亚 (Paigelynne Gonyea)，就社交媒体帖子与她对质。
戈尼亚认为，这一警告源于在一篇贴有枪杀古德的 ICE 官员乔纳森·罗斯 (Jonathan Ross) 的照片的帖子中写道，“我认为今天是乔纳森被起诉的好日子”。她在一月份发布了这篇文章，当时罗斯已经被新闻媒体确认了。
美国国土安全部发言人劳伦·比斯 (Lauren Bis) 分享了一张来自 Gonyea 的不同社交媒体帖子的图片，她说 Gonyea 分享了罗斯的地址。该帖子的部分内容已被编辑。
比斯在 6 月的一份声明中表示，Gonyea“通过在网上发布 ICE 执法人员的地址而犯下了联邦罪行”，并且“如果你对我们的官员进行人肉搜索，我们将调查你，并将你绳之以法。”
纽约总检察长办公室的一名代表表示，该办公室了解这两名居民与联邦特工的联系。该代表表示，该办公室一直在审查戈尼亚和联邦特工在投票期间的互动。

item.title: 爱达荷州一位母亲称她的双胞胎孩子在接种疫苗后死亡，她被指控谋杀
item.description:
当局称，爱达荷州一名妇女称，她的双胞胎双胞胎去年在接种疫苗后死亡，她面临与死亡有关的谋杀指控。
根据法庭记录和帕耶特警察局的一份声明，大陪审团于 6 月 29 日对安德里亚·肖 (Andrea Shaw) 提出两项一级谋杀罪，她被控于 2025 年 5 月窒息其 18 个月大的双胞胎。
去年，肖在由卫生与公共服务部部长小罗伯特·F·肯尼迪创立的反疫苗组织“儿童健康防御”制作的网络节目中表示，她的双胞胎在接种疫苗后死亡。肯尼迪自 2024 年 12 月正式辞去主席职务加入唐纳德·特朗普总统政府以来，就不再与该组织有任何关系。
23 岁的肖于周二被博伊西警察逮捕，并于周四被提审。她被交纳了 200 万美元的保释金，如果罪名成立或承认一级谋杀罪，她可能面临终身监禁或死刑。她的下一次出庭时间是 7 月 14 日。
肖的代表律师乔·菲利塞蒂在短信中写道，她“否认一切”，并且国家“无法证明”刑事指控。
“我们将全心全意地捍卫她，”菲利塞蒂补充道。
帕耶特警察局和帕耶特县检察官办公室周一拒绝发表评论。
2025 年 5 月，肖在儿童健康保卫节目中露面时说，她发现她的双胞胎在接种流感和其他疾病疫苗几天后死在自己的房间里。
“两名护士同时给他们注射了疫苗，”肖说。“然后他们就生病了。”
医学专家指出，有争议的儿童疫苗——甲型肝炎疫苗、流感疫苗和百白破疫苗——对儿童来说是安全有效的，并得到了各个医疗团体的推荐。
肖还是儿童健康保护组织和其他组织对美国儿科学会提起的联邦诉讼的原告。该诉讼于一月份向华盛顿联邦法院提起，指控美国儿科学会“在一家企业中发挥核心作用，几十年来在儿童疫苗接种计划的安全性问题上欺骗美国家庭”，从而敲诈勒索。在诉讼中，肖被描述为一位母亲，“她的孩子在按照美国儿科学会指南进行常规疫苗接种后死亡”。
美国儿科学会已要求法院驳回诉讼，并在 4 月份的一份法庭文件中声称，这是“针对”该学会及其“在疫苗政策中使用科学支持的证据”的“运动中的最新信件”。
一月份，当美国卫生官员对儿童疫苗指南进行了广泛修改，放弃了几项通用建议时，儿科医生和其他专家感到震惊。多年来帮助领导反疫苗运动的肯尼迪表示，这些变化更好地使美国与其他国家保持一致，“同时加强透明度和知情同意”。
今年三月，一名联邦法官阻止了这些改变，并表示肯尼迪可能违反了修改关键疫苗咨询委员会的联邦程序。但法官的命令并不是最终决定；这些封锁是暂时的，等待审判或简易判决的决定。
凯勒蒂从凤凰城报道。美联社路易斯维尔撰稿人劳拉·昂加尔 (Laura Ungar) 对此作出了贡献。

item.title: 在缅因州候选人面临性侵犯指控后，民主党开始撤回普拉特纳的支持
item.description:
A woman who previously dated Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner said he drunkenly forced her to have sex after she told him to stop, according to aPolitico reportreleased Monday, leading prominent supporters to pull their endorsements and throwing a must-win race for the party into turmoil.
Platner denied the allegation, but said he would be considering next steps for his campaign.
“Regardless of the inaccuracy of the reporting but mindful of the political reality it will inflict, we’re taking the time to reflect on the best path forward,” he said in a video released on social media.
Jenny Racicot, who lives in Maine, told Politico that Platner entered her home in 2021 while drunk and assaulted her. Racicot said she had been in an on-and-off relationship with Platner, but she cut off contact with him after that night and told him the incident wasn’t consensual. A voicemail left at a number listed for Racicot seeking comment did not receive an immediate response, but she said in a CNN interview on Monday evening that she opted not to fight back for fear of Platner, a former Marine, becoming more violent.
“He violated multiple layers of consent that night,” Racicot said.
Platner’s campaign did not immediately respond to an email and phone message from The Associated Press seeking comment.
“Any accusation of non-consensual behavior is categorically false,” Platner said in his video.
Platner won the Democratic nomination last month, setting himself up to face Republican Sen. Susan Collins, who has beaten back previous attempts to dislodge her from the seat that she’s held for nearly three decades.
Although Platner has long been controversial, the sexual allegation sparked a flight away from the candidate, who canceled a handful of town hall events. The main campaign arm of Senate Democrats called on Platner to drop out and said it would spend no money on the race, which is considered critical to control of the chamber, if he is the nominee.
“Graham Platner needs to immediately withdraw as the Democratic nominee for Senate and allow Maine Democrats the opportunity to choose a new candidate who can defeat Susan Collins,” Kirsten Gillibrand, chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, and Chuck Schumer, the top Senate Democrat, said in a joint statement.
The Democratic National Committee sent out an email soliciting money for Senate races hours after the Politico report posted, but Maine was not one of them. Ken Martin, the party chair, said, “Maine Democrats should select a new nominee.”
Rep. Ro Khanna, a California Democrat who stood by Platner even as the candidate faced previous controversies, said Monday’s allegation was enough. “I’ve been very clear that sexual assault or violence against women is a red line,” Khanna said. “These allegations are very serious and credible. Graham Platner should drop out from the race. I am withdrawing my endorsement.”
Arizona Sen. Ruben Gallego and Massachusetts Sen. Elizabeth Warren announced they were pulling their endorsements and called on Platner to drop out. The Democratic leaders of Maine’s legislature and top officials at the state Democratic Party did the same.
“This Senate race comes at a pivotal moment in the struggle against a government, supported by Senator Collins, that serves the interests of the wealthy and powerful at the expense of ordinary Maine people. It is essential that we refocus this campaign on that struggle,” party chair Charlie Dingman, vice chair Imke Schessler and executive director Devon Murphy-Anderson said in a joint statement.
“These allegations are appalling,” she said. “Nevertheless, it is not up to me to choose the Democratic nominee for Senate.”
State lawallows Platner to be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws by July 13. The replacement candidate must be named by July 27.
The Associated Press generally does not name victims of sexual assault, but in this case Racicot spoke in an interview with Politico.
Platner had never before held elected office, and Democratic leaders in Washington preferred Gov. Janet Mills in the primary. However, Mills, 78, dropped out as Platner, 41, consolidated support with help from progressive leaders at a time when Democratic voters have grown disenchanted with the party establishment.
While some Democratscame around to support himafter his commanding primary win, Platner’s controversial history had already left others openly despairing of their chances of winning the race. A veteran who also worked for a private security contractor, Platner has a chest tattoorecognized as a Nazi symbol, reportedlysexted with other womenshortly after getting married and had a history of inflammatory comments on social media.
In 2013, Platner posted on Reddit that people shouldn’t get so drunk “they wind up having sex with someone they don’t mean to” and that sexual assault victims should “just take some responsibility for themselves.” He’s since apologized for the post and says he no longer holds those beliefs.
The New York Times also reported that Platner had volatile relationships with previous girlfriends, one of whom saidan argument became physical.Platner denied the allegation.
Hasan Piker, a leftist commentator and streamer who backed Platner, seemed to reverse himself Monday following the Politico report.
“If new evidence presents itself, I’m going to change my perspective — it’s that simple,” Piker said during a livestream on Twitch, adding: “This is a clear-cut instance of verifiable sexual assault allegations. It’s completely irredeemable.”
Our Revolution, a progressive organization founded by Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont, said Platner should withdraw because the allegations “are too serious to treat as a distraction from the campaign or the issues.”
It also hinted at the potential battle over who would replace Platner.
“Whoever leads this movement forward must be someone who has actually lived the fight Graham Platner ran on: a record with working people, with unions, against corporate money, already tested and trusted by the same base that delivered this result,” said a statement from Joseph Geevarghese, executive director of Our Revolution.
Platner had pitched himself as a blue-collar oysterman and veteran who could reach disaffected voters. But as controversies mounted, some state Democrats had heartburn, embodied by Mills’ refusal to endorse Platner after she dropped out of the primary. Chatter circulated about possible replacements, including former state senator and logger Troy Jackson and Secretary of State Shenna Bellows.
“I’ve known this has been coming,” said Marie Follayttar, a Democrat and community organizer in Maine, talking about the growing whispers inside the state’s small population that had been bracing for yet another revelation surrounding Platner. “I’ve been scared and I’ve been sick waiting.”
Mike Connelly, a business owner and Democrat in Brunswick, Maine, said in an interview that he wants Platner to drop out after the latest allegations. But Connelly said he’d vote for him if he stays in.
“I would vote for a comatose Democrat before I would vote for Susan Collins,” Connelly said.
Jesse Bedayn contributed from Austin, Texas, Meg Kinnard contributed from Columbia, S.C., and Ali Swenson contributed from New York.

item.title: 参议员麦康奈尔正在“继续康复”，但在长期住院后细节很少
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——肯塔基州参议员米奇·麦康奈尔的工作人员上周表示，参议员在参议院休会期间正在医院“继续康复”。但他的办公室没有透露这位前共和党领导人在为期数周的住院期间的病情细节，也没有透露他下周参议院返回时是否会在国会大厦的细节。
根据麦康奈尔办公室的一份声明，麦康奈尔于 6 月 14 日入院，仅表示他“正在接受优质的护理”。一周后的一份声明称，他那周不会投票。周四，一份新的声明称，他“继续进步”，并“感谢他在医院继续康复期间所获得的大量支持。”
此后，他的办公室没有发布任何最新消息，发言人周一也没有回复置评请求。
这位参议员近年来多次住院治疗，随后出现了未具体说明的健康问题，而参议院共和党人在中期选举前的最后几个月已经以微弱优势获得多数席位。麦康奈尔现年 84 岁，是历史上任职时间最长的参议院领袖，随后卸任。他的最后一个任期将于一月结束。
当麦康奈尔仍担任共和党领袖时，他于 2023 年 3 月因脑震荡入院，并在华盛顿一家酒店摔倒后错过了数周的工作。那年夏天，他回国后两次在新闻发布会上僵住了，在同事和工作人员前来帮助他之前，他茫然地凝视着前方。一年后，他在走出共和党午餐会时摔倒并扭伤了手腕。
麦康奈尔在童年时期就患有小儿麻痹症，他很早就承认成年后走路和爬楼梯有些困难。2019年，他还在肯塔基州的家中绊倒，并接受了肩部骨折的手术。
麦康奈尔于 1984 年首次当选参议院议员，并从 2007 年到去年担任共和党领袖，在此期间担任多数党和少数党领袖。作为一名普通参议员，他一直很活跃，在众议院开会时出席工作，经常使用轮椅出行。

item.title: 欧足联表示国际足联让巴洛贡参加世界杯的决定“令人难以理解”
item.description:
GENEVA (AP) — Soccer leaders questioned theWorld Cup’s integrity on a chaotic and unprecedented day in the event’s modern history Monday.
The furor centered on a phone call thatPresident Donald Trumpmade last week to FIFA head Gianni Infantino to make the case that U.S. striker Folarin Balogun should not have been suspended for Monday’s matchup with Belgium because of a red card in a game last week. FIFA lifted the suspension and cleared Balogun to play.
The decision ultimately didn’t help the U.S. team, which was eliminated from the World Cup with a 4-1 loss to Belgium late Monday with Balogun in the lineup.
The decision appeared to be the first time since 1962 that punishment for a World Cup offense was suspended in the tournament, increasing scrutiny on Infantino’s control of FIFA and his close association with Trump.
European soccer body UEFA said FIFA “crossed a red line” and called Sunday’s decision by FIFA’s disciplinary committee “unprecedented, incomprehensible and unjustifiable.” Infantino denied having a role in the decision. Trump called it a “horrible” call and took credit for getting FIFA to review the foul, but said he did not demand an outcome.
The Belgian Football Association said it informed the U.S. Soccer Federation it was contesting Balogun’s eligibility.
But FIFA’s appeals committeedismissed Belgium’s legal challengeless than eight hours before scheduled kickoff of the round of 16 match in Seattle. The appellate panel said Belgium had no standing to challenge the decision.
It was unclear whether Belgium could pursue an appeal to the Court of Arbitration for Sport in Switzerland.
Balogun’s red card was assessed by Brazilian referee Raphael Claus for stepping on an opponent’s ankle last Wednesday during the Americans’ 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina, triggering an automatic one-game suspension. Claus did not initially issue a red card but showed it after a video review.
FIFA’s disciplinary committee on Sunday provisionally lifted the suspension for one year and fined Balogun $40,000, which the USSF can pay.
European soccer officials reacted with outrage.
“When the certainty of rules is no longer guaranteed by its guardians, the integrity of the game is at stake and the credibility of a competition is undermined,” UEFA said in a statement.
“Sometimes rules are open to interpretation. In this case not,” it said. “When the certainty of rules is no longer guaranteed by its guardians, the integrity of the game is at stake and the credibility of a competition is undermined.”
UEFA has oftenclashed with Infantinoduring his decade in FIFA power.
Infantino’s predecessor Sepp Blatter, forced from office in 2015 in fallout from corruption scandals,posted Monday on social media:
“Red cards are not overturned by political phone calls,” said Blatter. “They are overturned by rules, evidence and independent bodies.”
The Swiss Football Association declared that the “credibility of the competition depends on clear rules that are applied consistently.”
Coaches speculated about the implications of the decision going forward.
“What about the next red card? What happens then?” Norway coach Ståle Solbakken said. “Is there going to be some committee somewhere that is going to take that card away? It’s a bad, bad, bad, bad, bad decision that will hurt the World Cup.”
England coach Thomas Tuchel wondered whether yellow cards to English defender Declan Rice and French midfielder Michael Olise could be reversed.
FIFA’s disciplinary committee defended its decision in a statement Monday.
“Reviewing the legal consequences of red cards in football is nothing new in the modern game,” it said. “In the majority of top-tier leagues belonging to UEFA-affiliated member associations the overturning of red cards is a common disciplinary measure, yet this has never raised concerns about crossing any `red line.’”
Trump on Monday called the referee’s decision a “horrible” call while admitting he was confused about the rules and punishment surrounding red cards.
“I didn’t think it was a foul,” Trump said. “I thought it was two great athletes that crashed into each other and got entangled.”
“All I did was ask for a review,” Trump said. “I didn’t say, ‘You have to do this.’”
Infantino issued a statement saying: “FIFA’s judicial bodies are independent. They operate autonomously.”
“I explained that there was an ongoing legal process involving FIFA’s independent judicial bodies and that the case would be decided in due course by the competent bodies,” he said of his conversation with Trump.
Infantino’s relationship with Trumppreviously sparked concern among soccer officials. European soccer leaders walked out of a FIFA Congress in Paraguay last year due to a three-hour delay caused by Infantino arriving late because he was with Trump in the Middle East.
After Infantino awarded Trump the first FIFA peace prize in December, Norway’s governing body filed a letter supporting an ethics complaint against Infantino that accused him of violating provisions in FIFA’s code of ethics requiring political neutrality.
Belgian officials prepared in Seattle through the night into Monday to get a hearing with a FIFA-appointed appeals judge, and their eventual defeat might not be the end.
“Regardless of the sporting outcome of the match,” the Belgian federation said, ”(we are) deeply concerned by the way these events have unfolded and will continue, in the hours, days and months ahead, to pursue every available avenue to uphold the fundamental principles of ethics, sporting fairness and the interests of football as a whole.”
Balogun was sent off directly for planting his cleated foot on an ankle of defender Tarik Muharemović.
That kind of challenge has been a routine red card all season in competitions worldwide, and Balogun could have expected a two-game ban for serious foul play under the FIFA disciplinary code.
Still, similar challenges by star players have gone unpunished at this World Cup — by Argentina’sLionel Messi against Algeriaand Morocco’s Achraf Hakimi vs. Brazil. Bernardo Silva of Portugal got just a yellow card against Congo.
“I think a yellow card would have been fair,”Balogun later suggested.
This World Cup has been remarkable for FIFA under Infantino seeming torewrite the norms of disciplinary actioneven before the tournament began.
A pattern of pardonsopened FIFA to suggestions of executive intervention in the statutory independence of its judicial bodies, including the disciplinary committee that formally reprieved Balogun.
Cristiano Ronaldo was cleared to play in Portugal’s opening World Cup game despite getting a red card for serious foul play in a qualifying game against Ireland last November. He struck an opponent with an elbow.
Ronaldo served his mandatory ban in Portugal’s final qualifying game but he was reprieved from an expected two-game ban because FIFA introduced the idea of probation. An imposed three-game ban was less meaningful as two games were deferred during a one-year probationary period.
At the opening game on June 11, South Africa’s Themba Zwane got a red card against Mexico for a similar offense to Ronaldo’s andFIFA imposed a three-game banwith no probation. Zwane did not play again at the World Cup.
Three players sent off in their teams’ qualifying games last year were surprisingly told by FIFA in May they could serve their bans in a future competition instead of at the World Cup, which was the long-standing norm.
Ecuador midfielder Moisés Caicedo, Argentina defender Nicolás Otamendi and Qatar defender Tarek Salman all had their bans waived for the World Cup.

item.title: 比利时足协质疑国际足联让福拉林·巴洛贡参加世界杯比赛的决定
item.description:
比利时足协希望国际足联就让美国前锋弗拉林·巴洛贡参加世界杯的决定做出解释，尽管他在上一场比赛中吃到了红牌。
比利时周一晚些时候将与美国队争夺四分之一决赛席位。
比利时足协（RBFA）表示，尚未收到“国际足联的决定或有关此事的任何解释。在这种情况下，它别无选择，只能质疑球员参加即将举行的比赛的资格。”
它没有具体说明打算在哪里对国际足联的决定提出上诉。
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普代表美国明星前锋巴洛贡进行了干预，巴洛贡的红牌禁赛被解除，允许他参加对阵比利时的比赛。
比利时足协表示，他们通过媒体报道了解到国际足联的举动，并向管理机构发出了一封信，要求提供决定副本以及对程序的解释。
“作为唯一的回应，国际足联向RBFA发出了一封信函，表示它认为这封信函构成上诉，并且已经任命了一名法官，而RBFA只有几个小时的时间来完成上诉，”它说。“国际足联没有提供任何信息。
RBFA 坚称，国际足联的规定规定，合理的决定必须首先传达给上诉人。
声明称：“虽然皇家足协只是寻求合法解释，但国际足联本身提出了上诉，并立即确保该决定被宣布不予受理。”“所有这一切都发生在国际足联同时拒绝回应皇家足协的合法要求的同时。”
在本届杯赛中打进三球的美国队明星前锋巴洛贡在周三2-0战胜波黑队的比赛中，因笨拙地踩到波黑队塔里克·穆哈雷莫维奇的右脚踝而吃到红牌，引发自动一场禁赛。
国际足联周日宣布取消16强比赛的停赛，这一非同寻常的举动引发了特朗普的赞扬和比利时队的愤怒。这似乎是自 1962 年以来第一次世界杯期间的红牌没有导致停赛。
比利时足协补充道：“无论这场比赛的结果如何，皇家足协都对事态的发展深感担忧，并将在未来的数小时、数天和数月内继续奋战，捍卫道德、公平竞争和整个足球利益的基本原则。”
国际足联的决定引起了欧盟委员会的批评，欧盟委员会是欧盟强大的执行机构，总部设在布鲁塞尔。
欧盟体育事务专员格伦·米卡莱夫表示，“有关体育规则和体育事务的决定属于体育机构，而不是政客”。
“影响体育决策将破坏体育的自主权，”他在 X 上的一条消息中写道。“我们的重点应该放在体育面临的真正治理挑战上，包括出于政治目的将体育武器化。”

item.title: 国际足联解除美国球星弗拉林·巴洛贡的禁赛后需要了解什么
item.description:
FIFA’sstunning decisionto lift the suspension of a star U.S. player riled the host country’s World Cup opponent, Belgium, and sent soccer fans — and political leaders — into a frenzy over the influence President Donald Trump may have had over the extremely rare ruling.
Hours before kickoff Monday, FIFA dismissedBelgium’s challengeto the most-debated political intervention in aWorld Cupin decades. U.S. forward Folarin Balogun had faced a mandatory ban from the match after receiving a red card last week, but FIFA lifted his suspension on Sunday followinga call Trump madeto the global soccer organization’spresident, Gianni Infantino.
Despite Balogun’s start in Seattle, the U.S. lost 4-1 to Belgium.
Born in New York to Nigerian parents, raised in London, and playing in the French league, Balogun’sbirthright citizenshipmade him eligible for the U.S.
The Belgian soccer federation wants an explanation from FIFA about a decision to let U.S forward Folarin Balogun play at the World Cup despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium takes on the United States later Monday for a spot in the quarterfinals.
Securing his commitment to play on the American team was a coup and it had paid off; the 25-year-old led the team’s World Cup scoring with three goals.
All was well until Wednesday when hestepped onopponent Tarik Muharemović’s ankle in a2-0 winover Bosnia-Herzegovina in the round of 32.
The decision to send off Balogunwas disputed— his movement seemed clumsy but not malicious. But once a referee decides to issue a red card, the punishment is usually straightforward: The player is excluded from the rest of the game and — until now, at least — a suspension for the next game is automatic.
If the ban had stayed in place, replacing Balogun in the lineup posed a big challenge for coach Mauricio Pochettino.
The U.S. had plenty of attacking players in wider or deeper roles, but few with the combination of physical power and goal-scoring ability for the center-forward role that the rest of the offense focuses around. Likely replacement Ricardo Pepi hadn’t scored in four World Cup games.
There is typically no appeals process against the automatic one-game ban, only for longer sanctions usually applied to the most serious fouls like violent conduct or racism.
In its decision to let Balogun play against Belgium, FIFA cited article 27 of its disciplinary code, which says a “judicial body” can “fully or partially suspend the implementation of a disciplinary measure.” Balogun could yet get that one-game suspension on top of any future punishment if he commits a similar offense again in the next year.
In a statement Monday, FIFA’s disciplinary committee gave more details on its decision. It said it found Balogun guilty of two breaches of its disciplinary code: one related to the red card and another for celebrating with his teammates on the field after the Bosnia match despite having been ejected. It imposed a fine of $40,000 but suspended the automatic one-match ban for a probationary period of one year.
“The sanction remains dormant during the probationary period and will only be activated if he commits another infringement of a similar nature and gravity during that one-year period,” the committee said.
Infantino insisted in a social media post that FIFA’s disciplinary committee acted with independence and judged cases such as Balogun’s on “applicable regulations and the specific facts.” Article 27 doesn’t lay out any requirements for which cases are eligible under the rarely used rule.
Last year, FIFA suspended two games of a three-game ban for one of soccer’s biggest-ever stars, Cristiano Ronaldo. That left himfree to play in the opening games of the World Cupfor Portugal. He did serve the remaining one game ban in a qualifier.
Balogun’s case seems to be the first since 1962 in which a sending-off during a World Cup match didn’t result in a suspension. On that occasion, the president of host nation Chile argued for Brazilian midfielder Garrincha to be allowed to play the final after he had kicked a Chilean opponent.
“Thank you to FIFA for doing what was right, and reversing a great injustice!” Trump said Sunday on social media after Balogun’s suspension was lifted. On Monday, Trump defended his outreach to Infantino, saying he merely pointed out that the referee’s call against Balogun seemed like a bad one and warranted a closer look.
Infantino and Trump have developed awell-known relationship. The Swiss soccer official became a regular visitor to the Oval Office as the U.S. prepared to host the World Cup. He gave Trump aFIFA peace prizeat the World Cup draw in December, an award the organization hasn’t presented to anyone else before or since.
FIFA’s statutes prohibit governments from intervening in the independence of soccer bodies managing their own affairs. FIFA regularly suspends member federations where governments have interfered in decision-making.
Pochettino, the U.S. coach, applauded FIFA’s move Sunday and said the initial on-field ruling against Balogun was “completely unfair.”
The Belgian soccer federation said it was “astonished” when the news of FIFA’s intervention broke. Coach Rudi Garcia likened the decision to April Fools’ Day.
On Monday afternoon, a FIFA appeals judge dismissed Belgium’s legal challenge fewer than eight hours before kickoff. The Belgian soccer body “is not a party to the proceedings and, as such, has no standing to appeal the decision,”FIFA said in a statement.
FIFA’s disciplinary code says suspensions of two games or less typically can’t be appealed — though that would generally apply to teams wanting a suspension lifted, not reimposed.
As Europe woke to the news Monday, the Instagram account of Belgian Prime Minister Bart de Wever’s cat, Maximus — a social media celebrity in his own right — weighed in with apicturecaptioned: “Red card? I’m still going to play!”
European soccer body UEFAcriticized FIFAfor an “incomprehensible and unjustifiable decision” and warned “the integrity of the game is at stake.”
In an apparent response to UEFA’s criticism, FIFA’s disciplinary committee said “reviewing the legal consequences of red cards in football is nothing new in the modern game.”
Norway coach Ståle Solbakken weighed in after his team stunned Brazil on Sunday to reach the quarterfinals.
“What about the next red card? What happens then?” he said. “Is there going to be some committee somewhere that is going to take that card away?”
Former England great Wayne Rooney said on the BBC: “Infantino, he should be ashamed of this because I think the sportsmanship of this game is in question here.”
Ex-Sweden striker Zlatan Ibrahimović was a prominent voice welcoming the decision.
“First of all, he should not get a red card and then they should have come quicker, this call,” Ibrahimovic said on Fox Sports. “I’m happy for the U.S. team because the U.S. team has been amazing but Balo has been super-amazing.”
England coach Thomas Tuchel predicted this could set off a flood of complaints and appeals over other on-field decisions affecting key players at the World Cup.
“Where to draw the line is the question that I ask,” he said after England beat Mexico 3-2 for a quarterfinal spot.
“Our yellow card from the first minute against Declan Rice, we can now debate endlessly. I think it’s not a yellow card. Do we get this back?”
AP Sports Writer Graham Dunbar in Geneva contributed to this report.

item.title: 乔纳森·安德森 (Jonathan Anderson) 将迪奥 (Dior) 的热度从泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 的隐形婚纱转向雕塑般的高级定制时装
item.description:
巴黎（美联社）——乔纳森·安德森 (Jonathan Anderson) 抵达巴黎时装周，时尚界仍在等待他为泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 制作的迪奥 (Dior) 婚纱。
周一，第一天，他试图给它一些别的东西来看待。
斯威夫特在纽约麦迪逊广场花园与美国国家橄榄球联盟 (NFL) 明星特拉维斯·凯尔斯 (Travis Kelce) 结婚三天后，两人均身着迪奥 (Dior) 礼服，安德森 (Anderson) 带着受美国艺术家琳达·本格里斯 (Lynda Benglis) 启发的雕塑般的重褶高级时装系列重返秀台。
对于 LVMH 旗下品牌和 41 岁的北爱尔兰设计师安德森来说，这一委托是一个重大举措。安德森一年前被任命负责彻底改革迪奥的所有时装系列。
几个月来，行业观察家一直把赌注押在美国名字上，比如拉尔夫·劳伦(Ralph Lauren)，或者斯威夫特经常穿的薇薇安·韦斯特伍德(Vivienne Westwood)。
世界希望看到的一件衣服是安德森不会展示的一件。
所以周一他改变了话题——艺术。
该系列试图将话题从斯威夫特的隐藏礼服转移到 Benglis 的作品上，Benglis 自 20 世纪 60 年代末以来以将乳胶倒在画廊地板上，让金属折叠和下垂成形而闻名。
迪奥工作室被视为她工作室的一个版本——平面织物被压制、打结和弯曲成三个维度的地方。
Benglis 将扁平材料弯曲成型；最终，高级时装也是如此。
衣服也遵循了这个想法。银箔花瓣裙随着每一步而移动。
无肩带银色金属丝长袍上系着一个超大蝴蝶结。裤子和衬衫均采用手工压紧的褶皱制成。
Dior 标志性的 Bar 夹克是该品牌自 1947 年以来一直沿用的高腰款式，经过多种改造：采用带有磨边流苏的蕨绿色花呢，采用折叠成巨大蝴蝶结的灰色千鸟格，还有一次在下摆处采用宽松的雪纺线。
其他造型完全由刺绣丝花打造而成。
一件蓝色薄纱的大扇子铺在一件连衣裙的前面。
手袋采用金属褶皱设计，其中四个是 Benglis 亲自设计的。
法国又迎来了另一场热浪，气温超过 30 摄氏度（86 华氏度）。
迪奥向粉丝们发出了邀请函，嘉宾们在罗丹博物馆花园的时装秀中使用了邀请函。
前排混杂着流行歌星和艺术家。
歌手萨布丽娜·卡彭特和演员乔什·奥康纳坐在嘉宾中，其中包括佩丽冉卡·乔普拉、尼克·乔纳斯、娜奥米·沃茨、丽贝卡·弗格森和艾里珊·钟。
尽管令人兴奋，安德森的赌注很简单：世界上最著名的时装屋可以承受奇怪的感觉。
人们经常将他与竞争对手 Chanel 的新设计师 Matthieu Blazy 相比较，后者本月为歌手 Dua Lipa 设计了婚纱。
这个季节现在有一个独特的特点：它的两个最大的故事是不允许任何人看到的连衣裙。
正如时装传统所规定的那样，这场秀以新娘结束。
安德森推出了一件浅色无肩带长裙，上面覆盖着手工褶皱雪纺长面纱，饰有蒲公英羽毛和仙人掌花刺绣。
这是安德森本周展示的第二件婚纱，也是唯一一件任何人都可以拍摄的婚纱。

item.title: 乔治·克鲁尼荣获威尼斯电影节终身成就奖
item.description:
乔治·克鲁尼今年将重返威尼斯电影节，并获得终身成就奖。电影节组织者周一表示，克鲁尼已被选为 9 月第 83 届电影节金狮奖获得者。
“我在威尼斯度过了很多非凡的时刻。毫无疑问，这个节日是我最喜欢的，获得金狮奖是一种巨大的荣誉，”克鲁尼在一份声明中说。“这也可能意味着我老了，但我会接受。”
这位明星确实在威尼斯和电影节上有过许多令人难忘的亮相，包括 1998 年史蒂文·索德伯格 (Steven Soderbergh) 改编的埃尔莫·伦纳德 (Elmore Leonard) 改编的《视线之外》(Out of Sight)，以及 2005 年他作为导演的第二部影片《晚安，好运》(Good Night, and Good Luck)。 去年，他出演了诺亚·鲍姆巴赫 (Noah Baumbach) 的《杰·凯利》(Jay Kelly)，在片中他扮演一位正在经历生存危机的电影明星，准备接受一生。意大利电影节成就奖。
电影节主席阿尔贝托·巴伯拉在一份声明中表示：“身兼演员、导演和制片人三重身份的乔治·克鲁尼是一位完整而富有魅力的艺术家，充满激情和原创性，他将一种深刻的职业转变为当代电影中最明亮的抛物线之一。”“这位演员完美地结合了昔日的明星魅力、卓越的专业精神和现代敏感性，以罕见的多才多艺跨越了不同的流派。”
2014 年，克鲁尼与当时的阿迈勒·阿拉姆丁在威尼斯交换了誓言。
第83届威尼斯电影节于9月2日开幕，持续至9月12日。

item.title: 被特朗普解雇的前民权机构专员在最高法院裁决后放弃诉讼
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — A former Democratic commissioner of one of the country’s top civil rights agencies dropped a lawsuit Mondaychallenging her dismissalby President Donald Trump, citing a recentSupreme Court rulingthat dramatically enhanced the president’s power over independent agencies.
Trump’s unprecedented dismissal of Jocelyn Samuels and another Democrat from the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission cleared the way for his shake-up of civil rights enforcement, which has prioritized rooting outdiversity and inclusion practices, weakening protections fortransgender workersand championing discrimination claimsagainst white workersand U.S.-born workers.
The EEOC moved forward with aspects of that plan Monday, releasinga regulatory agendathat includes proposals to end its annual collection of workplace demographic data and rescind longstanding guidance warning it may be discriminatory to require workers to exclusively use English on the job, among other changes.
One of Trump’s first acts as president was demolishing the Democratic majority on the normally five-member EEOC, sweeping away what would have been a major obstacle to his civil rights agenda.His dismissal of Samuels and Charlotte Burrowsbefore the end of their five-year terms was unprecedented in the history of the EEOC, which was created by Congress through the 1964 Civil Rights Act.
The EEOC commission now consists of two Republicans and one Democrat. Trump has yet to nominate candidates to fill two open seats on the commission.
In her lawsuit, Samuels had argued that Congress established that the EEOC commissioners, who are appointed by the president and confirmed by the Senate, would serve staggered terms to ensure “continuity, stability and insulation from political pressure.”
But in a statement Monday, Samuels said she was dismissing her lawsuit because the Supreme Court’s ruling in a separate case “leaves me without a viable path forward to continue contesting my termination.”
Last week, the Supreme Court upheld Trump’s firing of the heads of independent agencies, with the exception of the Federal Reserve, jettisoning a 91-year-old decision that had limited when presidents can fire board members of independent agencies.
EEOCChair Andrea Lucasapplauded the Supreme Court’s ruling ina LinkedIn postlast week, saying that it reinforced that the “EEOC is an executive branch agency.”
In a statement Monday about the new regulatory agenda, an EEOC spokesperson said the agency is “committed to implementing President Trump’s landmark civil rights agenda, dedicated to evenhanded enforcement of federal civil rights laws.”
The EEOC’s sole Democratic commissioner, Kalpana Kotagal, voted against the agenda, saying in a statement that “the proposed changes weaken civil rights protections for workers and undermine the agency’s investigative and enforcement efforts.”
The regulatory agenda includes a proposal to end a 40-year requirement for companies with 100 or more employees, or federal contractors with at least 50 workers, to submit workforce demographic data to the EEOC.
Lucas has publicly warned companies not to use such demographic data to justify what she describes as potentially discriminatory practices to bolster the diversity for their workforce. In its proposed rescission, the EEOC said requirements impose “significant financial and administrative burdens on the nation’s employers.” The rescission will almost certainly be approved by the EEOC commission but it will then be subject to a public commentary period before final passage.
Conservative critics have said demographic data encourages the EEOC to assume that discrimination is behind any gender or racial imbalance in a company’s workforce. Supporters say the EEOC has used the annual surveys to help identify discriminatory patterns, guide its priorities and help track how women and minorities have fared since the passage of the Civil Rights Act.
“The EEOC has collected this data from employers for six decades. It’s difficult to understand why the agency would kneecap its ability to investigate discrimination, particularly at a time when the EEOC is chronically understaffed and underfunded,” Kotagal said.
The EEOC also announced its intent to rescind 1980 guidelines defining national origin-based discrimination, which warns employers that any requirement for workers to exclusively speak English may “create an atmosphere of inferiority, isolation and intimidation based on national origin which could result in a discriminatory working environment.” The EEOC argued that the guidelines are outdated and incorrectly established a “presumption that English-only rules violate Title VII in some circumstances.”
Last week, the EEOC voted to toss out longstanding guidelines on what sort of voluntary affirmative action employers can take to improve job opportunities for women and minorities without violating Title VII of the 1964 Civil Rights Act, which prohibits employers from making employment decisions based on race, color, national origin, sex and religion. The EEOC reversed its stance that employers could pursue some programs, such as training for women and minorities or steps to expand recruitment pools, without running afoul of Title VII.
Also on the EEOC’s agenda is revising regulations for enforcing thePregnant Workers Fairness Act, a law that entitles women to seek workplace accommodations for pregnancy and related medical conditions. Lucas opposed the Biden-era regulations for including abortion as a circumstance allowing accommodations, such as time off for medical appointments.
The Associated Press’ women in the workforce coverage receives financial support from Pivotal Ventures. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP’sstandardsfor working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas atAP.org.

item.title: 微软因游戏部门“重组”而裁员 4,800 人，其中包括 Xbox 部门的许多员工
item.description:
华盛顿州雷德蒙德（美联社）——微软将裁员 4,800 人，约占其全球员工的 2.1%，其中包括 Xbox 视频游戏业务的大量员工。
该公司周一表示，此次裁员包括 1,600 名 Xbox 员工，今年还将进行更广泛的重组，旨在“重置”Xbox，因为它面临着激烈的竞争。
今年早些时候接管游戏部门的 Xbox 首席执行官阿莎·夏尔马 (Asha Sharma) 在一份备忘录中表示：“我们今天的业务并不健康。”“我们的运营利润率比同类平台和出版业务低 3-10 倍。”
夏尔马表示，Xbox 与索尼的 PlayStation 和任天堂的 Switch 竞争的行业正面临着严重的“硬件危机”，因为游戏机组件的成本飙升。
Sharma 表示，除了周一宣布的裁员之外，Xbox 预计在上周开始的财年中将再裁员 1,600 人。该公司还剥离了之前被微软收购的四个视频游戏开发工作室。
大约三年前，微软以 690 亿美元完成了收购游戏巨头动视暴雪 (Activision Blizzard) 的交易，动视暴雪是《使命召唤》和其他热门系列游戏的开发商。该公司当时表示，希望扩大其游戏开发产品组合，并提供类似 Netflix 的流媒体订阅服务，但该策略似乎不足以在竞争中领先。
夏尔马表示：“虽然这些业务创造了有意义的价值，但它们的增长速度并未达到我们预期。”
Xbox 裁员是微软公司首席人力官艾米·科尔曼 (Amy Coleman) 与客户需求的未具体变化联系在一起的更广泛裁员的补充。
科尔曼在博客文章中写道：“我还想直接表明，今天被淘汰的角色不会被人工智能取代。”
此次裁员是在微软 5 月份开始向约 8,750 名员工提供自愿买断后进行的。科尔曼周一表示，超过 30% 的符合条件的工人接受了这些自愿退休方案。

item.title: Sky同意以21亿美元收购英国广播公司ITV，与流媒体巨头竞争
item.description:
伦敦（美联社）——康卡斯特旗下的英国电视、互联网和移动电话提供商 Sky，经过数月的谈判，同意以高达 16 亿英镑（合 21 亿美元）的价格收购 ITV 的媒体和娱乐部门，以打造全球流媒体巨头的主要竞争对手。
该交易包括英国最大商业广播公司 ITV 的地面电视频道和流媒体服务。以《我是名人》系列节目而闻名的 ITV 制作部门 ITV Studios 将仍然是一家独立公司。
ITV 董事长 Andrew Cosslett 在一份声明中表示，此次交易将“打造一家拥有规模和资源的英国冠军企业，以便更好地与全球流媒体平台竞争”。
ITV 表示，在完成与康卡斯特的分拆后，合并后的 Sky-ITV 实体将成为 NBCUniversal 的一部分。
